channel.title: The Jiffy Book Club: Glorious Country
channel.description: A six-part audio companion to The Jiffy Book Club's inaugural selection: "Glorious Country: How the Artist Frederic Church Brought the World to America and America to the World," by Victoria Johnson. Produced by The Jiffy, an independent newsletter and podcast covering the arts and sciences of upstate New York, and hosted by James Cave.
channel.itunes:author: James Cave

image.title: The Jiffy Book Club: Glorious Country

item.title: 1. The Charter Oak
item.description: Welcome to the Jiffy Book Club audio feed for our inaugural season featuring "Glorious Country." Here, we learn about the book, the series, and what to expect over the next six weeks.
item.itunes:title: The Invitation

item.title: 2. The World
item.description: The geography, history, and cultural landscape behind Glorious Country — the Hudson Valley that shaped Frederic Church, and the world Victoria Johnson reconstructs.
item.itunes:title: The World

item.title: 3. The Marginalia
item.description: Notes from the margins — passages, observations, and threads worth sitting with as you read.
item.itunes:title: The Marginalia

item.title: 4. The Jaunt Dispatch
item.description: A field report from Olana and the Church sightlines of the Hudson Valley — companion audio to the Jiffy Jaunt itinerary for Glorious Country season.
item.itunes:title: The Jaunt Dispatch

item.title: 5. The Conversation
item.description: James Cave in conversation with Victoria Johnson, author of Glorious Country. Cross-posted from The Jiffy podcast feed.
item.itunes:title: The Conversation

item.title: 6. The Coda
item.description: A closing note on Glorious Country season — what we read, where we went, and what stays with us.
item.itunes:title: The Coda
